corrections.issue: Raw item repeated the Medigap 30-day free-look angle already validated in an earlier batch.
corrections.action: Replaced with a Texas long-term care 30-day free-look angle while preserving the timing concept.

corrections.issue: Monthly grace-period angle would have duplicated a prior validated batch if left unchanged.
corrections.action: Validated the fresh legal-actions timing angle instead.

corrections.issue: Raw item used an unsupported 31-day post-COBRA conversion framing.
corrections.action: Replaced with the supported COBRA conversion-option rule requiring the option to be offered during the 180-day period ending on COBRA expiration if such a conversion option is otherwise generally available under the plan.

corrections.issue: Raw item overstated that the insurer is always the sole ultimately responsible party for the content of an advertisement.
corrections.action: Replaced with the supported Texas advertising rule that an advertisement must identify the person or entity responsible for it.

notable_checks.note: Electronic clean claims from preferred providers generally require action/payment within 30 days; nonelectronic clean claims within 45 days.
Expired general-lines license renewals within 90 days require the renewal fee plus an additional fee equal to one-half of the required renewal fee, with CE completed.
Reinstated health policies cover accidental injury after reinstatement and sickness beginning more than 10 days after reinstatement.

cross_batch_review: Performed targeted duplicate-angle review against validated batches 01–07. Approval-blocking repeats were corrected in B08-Q014, B08-Q018, and B08-Q024. No unresolved approval-blocking duplicate-angle issues remain.
